titleStmt.title: Letter from William J. Thompson to Sir Matthew Nathan, 21 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William J Thompson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from the Registrar General of Ireland, Sir William John Thompson (1861â€“1929) to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) the Under Secretary to the Chief Crown Secretary of Ireland Augustine Birrell (1850-1933). In the letter Thompson is asking Nathan whether or not a case involving Birth Certificate fraud would be re-tried.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3908

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
General Register Office, Charlemont House, DUBLIN , 21st January, 1916 . Letter of 20. 8. 1915. No. 13646. Sir, With reference to previous correspondence, in the case of
Mr.John Graham who was proceeded against on the 1st ultimo at
the Assizes at Belfast on a charge of forgery in connection with
the issue of a false Certificate of Birth, which resulted in a
disagreement of the Jury, may I request you will be so good as
to inform me whether this case is to be again tried. I am, Sir,
Your obedient Servant, William J. Thompson
Registrar General. The Under Secretary &c., &c., Dublin Castle
